hook: Why does keeping it together feel like a full-time job?
slide2: I woke up already tired today. Not sleepy tired. The other kind. The kind where you stare at your phone for 20 minutes before you can make yourself get up.
slide3: I keep replaying conversations from three days ago. Wondering if I said the wrong thing. Wondering if everyone secretly thinks I'm too much. I don't even know why my brain does this.
slide4: There's this version of me that smiles in reels and replies 'I'm good yaar' to everyone. And then there's this. Both are real. I don't know which one I am anymore.
closer: When did existing start feeling like so much effort?
caption:
Some days I'm holding it together with absolute zero energy left over for myself. Not sad exactly. Just... done pretending the weight isn't heavy.

Nobody warns you that functioning is its own kind of exhausting.

If you also live in your head too much and smile through it anyway — hi, same.

When's the last time you actually said 'I'm not okay' and meant it?
